name: Columbia Cascade Housing Corporations Housing Resource Center
description_short: Non-profit affordable housing developer serving low-income families, elderly, and disabled residents across Oregon and Washington's Mid-Columbia region for over 35 years.
description_long:
Columbia Cascade Housing Corporation is a non-profit organization that has operated as a local leader in affordable housing development for over 35 years. The organization was formed to address housing and community development needs of low-income families in a five-county bi-state region spanning Sherman, Wasco, and Hood River counties in Oregon, and Klickitat and Sanamania counties in Washington. Their mission centers on promoting adequate and affordable housing, economic opportunity, and suitable living environments free from discrimination for those with barriers due to income or disability.

The organization operates multiple residential communities offering one, two, three, and four-bedroom units tailored to different populations. Their portfolio includes Carson Springs in Carson, Washington; West Park Orchards (55+ community) in The Dalles; Rio Bella and Hood River Crossing apartments in Hood River; WyEast Vista (agriculture and farmworker housing) in Hood River; and White Cap Apartments (disability-friendly) in Stevenson. They also operate Mariposa Village and manage Project-Based Voucher programs in partnership with the Mid-Columbia Housing Authority.

What distinguishes Columbia Cascade Housing is their focus on linking appropriate community services with affordable housing to promote independence and self-sufficiency. They emphasize how stable housing improves family outcomes—better school performance for children, improved health and mental health for adults, and greater ability to build savings and assets. Their bilingual website and community-specific properties (seniors, farmworkers, disability-friendly) demonstrate tailored approaches to diverse populations across rural and small urban settings.

As a housing provider rather than a financial services company, Columbia Cascade does not offer credit counseling, credit repair, or financial advisory services. They focus exclusively on providing affordable rental housing units and related housing support services. Prospective residents should contact them directly through their website for application information and availability details for specific properties.
pros: 35+ years of experience and established track record in affordable housing development across bi-state region
Multiple specialized properties addressing specific demographics: seniors (55+), farmworkers, and disability-friendly communities
Mission-driven non-profit structure ensures focus on service rather than profit maximization
Comprehensive property portfolio with various unit sizes (1-4 bedrooms) to accommodate different family sizes
Geographic diversity with locations across five counties in Oregon and Washington
Links community services with housing to promote resident independence and self-sufficiency
cons: No financial counseling or credit-building services available; purely housing-focused organization
Limited online information about current rental availability, waitlists, or application timelines
Website does not clearly display income qualification thresholds or rental pricing
No information about acceptance of subsidies, vouchers, or specific tenant support programs beyond project-based vouchers
Bilingual content limited to homepage; detailed property and application information appears primarily in English
best_for: Low-income families seeking long-term affordable rental housing in rural Oregon/Washington communities
Seniors (55+) seeking safe, stable, affordable housing with community services and accessibility
Agricultural and farmworker households in Hood River area needing multi-bedroom family housing
Disabled and elderly individuals needing accessible, supportive residential communities
diagnosis: Columbia Cascade Housing Corporation is best for low-income families, seniors, farmworkers, and disabled individuals seeking stable, affordable rental housing in the Mid-Columbia region. The primary caveat is that they provide housing services only—not consumer financial services, credit counseling, or financial advisory assistance; applicants seeking housing-related financial guidance should seek HUD-approved counselors separately.
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
